behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure can occur at the tool↔git boundary (e.g., wrong git diff flags so rename is emitted as D+A). REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: using mocked git output can hide real rename-detection behavior from git on an actual repo history. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic/Workflow are disqualified by behavior text (Python git CLI summary only; no AI model decisions). LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: an Integration test with stubbed git output would miss a real failure where subprocess invocation does not enable/receive rename detection.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temporary git repo, commit a file, rename it, run the CLI against that repo, and assert stdout report has the file only in the rename category and not in added/deleted counts.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: crash risk is often in real subprocess output/decoding paths when git reports binary changes. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: synthetic fixtures may not reproduce exact binary diff signaling/bytes from real git. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic/Workflow disqualified (no AI component in behavior text). LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Integration with canned text could pass while real execution fails on binary diff output handling.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a real git repo, add/modify a binary file (e.g., image), run the CLI, assert process exits successfully and report includes binary file count/category without traceback.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior depends on actual git behavior in an unborn-HEAD repo (real exit codes/messages). REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: mocking git responses can mask command/exit-code handling bugs. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic/Workflow disqualified by absence of AI steps. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Integration with fake repo service can miss real subprocess failure handling when git returns errors on no-commit repositories.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real empty git repo with no commits, execute the CLI, and verify stdout is an empty structured report and exit status is non-error.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this validates end-to-end correctness of history extraction from real git state across commits and file operations. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: stubs can validate parser math but not whether the CLI queries git history correctly in practice. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic/Workflow disqualified (no model output quality involved). LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Integration tests with fixture diffs could miss incorrect git command selection/range handling against a real repository timeline.
behaviors.test_description: Build a real repo with a controlled sequence of add/delete/rename/modify commits, run the CLI over the target range, and assert reported category counts equal the known ground truth from the constructed history.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: staged vs unstaged semantics live in real git index/working-tree state and command interactions. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: mocked repositories often collapse these states and can hide divergence bugs. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic/Workflow disqualified by behavior text (git/CLI only). LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Integration with fake status objects can miss real failures where the CLI uses different git commands/options for index vs working tree.
behaviors.test_description: In a real repo, create equivalent staged and unstaged change scenarios, run the CLI in each state, and assert identical structured stdout output.
